name: San Francisco Main Library

permits.description: erect a seven story public library-new main branch library
erect a seven story public library-new main branch library
removal of existing 42 atrium smoke exhaust fans and replacement with 42 new atrium smoke exhaust fans due to equipment obsolescence.
replacement of roofing membrane, replace sealant around skylights and curtainwall, replace plumbing drains as need on roof drains

scope consists of 1st floor renovation of main library. tenant improvments and installation of sorting machine, automated storage and retrievel machine by logistex (under different permit). no exterior work.

tenant improvement for teen ctr at 2/fl. selective interior demo of(e)bldg, construction of new offices,sound booth,video recording booth,maker space(arts&crafts) single user restroom w/updated mech,plumbing,elec sys. fire sprinkler and fire alarm revisions under separate application.

summary_en: The record centers on the making of the current Main Library. A 1991 draft environmental impact report assesses the New Main Library project on Marshall Square, and a facility assessment recommends new construction there after a 1986 space-requirements study. A 1991 press release notes the Library Commission approved design-development drawings for the new building, designed by Cathy Simon of Simon Martin-Vegue Winkelstein & Moris and James Ingo Freed. A 2000 post-occupancy evaluation reviews the finished building and its close-out items. Operated by the Public Library (FY2025 budget about $187M); a 'Workforce Dev Main Library' contract with Urban Alchemy has paid $2,702,896.81 of an agreed $5,011,265.61.
